name: sqlx (Go)
what_it_does: Extensions to Go's standard database/sql package. sqlx provides named query parameters, struct scanning (scan SQL rows directly into Go structs), batch operations, and safer query building on top of database/sql — without the full ORM abstraction. The middle ground between raw database/sql (too verbose) and GORM (too opinionated) for Go database access in agent services.
use_cases: Query databases in Go agent backends and scan results directly into structs without manual field mapping
Write named SQL queries in Go agent services using :name parameters instead of positional $1 markers
Perform batch inserts and updates in agent data pipelines with sqlx's named stmt support
Build Go agent data layers with raw SQL power and type safety without full ORM overhead
Execute complex SQL queries in agent analytics services that GORM can't express cleanly
not_for: Auto-migration and schema management — use GORM or golang-migrate for schema migrations
Developers who prefer ORM abstraction — GORM provides more convenience for CRUD operations
best_when: You want to write raw SQL in Go agent services but with struct scanning and named parameters — more control than GORM, less boilerplate than database/sql.
avoid_when: You need auto-migration, associations, or full ORM features — use GORM. You prefer complete type safety — use sqlc or ent.

auth.notes: Database auth via standard database/sql DSN. sqlx adds no auth layer.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — limited by database capacity

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard database/sql errors with driver-level messages. sql.ErrNoRows for missing records. StructScan errors include field name context for type mismatch.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Idempotency depends on SQL logic. sqlx provides no upsert abstraction — write upsert SQL explicitly.
known_agent_gotchas: sqlx.StructScan requires struct fields to have db: tags matching column names — missing tags cause fields to be silently skipped (not zero-valued)
Named queries (sqlx.Named) expand :name params — must prepare with db.PrepareNamed() not db.Prepare(); mixing prepared statement styles causes runtime errors
sqlx is a wrapper on database/sql — connection pooling uses database/sql's SetMaxOpenConns/SetMaxIdleConns; don't set separately
db.Select() loads all rows into memory — for large datasets use db.Queryx() with iteration to avoid memory issues in agent data pipelines
sqlx is in maintenance mode — no new features, only security patches; consider sqlc for new projects wanting compile-time SQL verification
Transactions with sqlx.Tx require using tx methods (not db methods) — passing db to functions inside transactions won't participate in the transaction

security_score_components.security_notes: Parameterized queries prevent SQL injection. Named queries are still parameterized. DSN credentials must use environment variables.
